description.task: Implement Counting Sort for non-negative integers. Count the frequency of each element, compute cumulative counts (to maintain stability), then place elements in sorted order into an output array.
instructions: Counting sort runs in O(n+k) where k is the max value — linear time!
It is only suitable when the range of values k is not too large relative to n
description.hint: Step1: count[x]++; Step2: count[i]+=count[i-1]; Step3: output[count[arr[i]]-1]=arr[i]; count[arr[i]]--;

description.task: Implement Radix Sort (Least Significant Digit) that sorts integers by individual digit positions. Use a stable Counting Sort on each digit place (1s, 10s, 100s, …). Process digits from least significant to most significant.
instructions: The countByDigit method is fully implemented — just add the loop in radixSort
Radix sort is O(d*(n+k)) where d=number of digits, k=10 for base-10 — effectively O(n) for fixed-width integers
Processing digits from LSD (exp=1) to MSD maintains correct order

description.task: Implement Bucket Sort for floating-point numbers in the range [0.0, 1.0). Distribute values into n buckets, sort each bucket using insertion sort, then concatenate. This achieves O(n) average time.
instructions: Sort each bucket: for(List<Double> b:buckets) Collections.sort(b);
Concatenate: for(List<Double> b:buckets) for(double x:b) arr[idx++]=x;
The output should be all 10 floats in ascending order
Bucket sort works best when input is uniformly distributed over a range
description.hint: Distribute: buckets[(int)(x*n)].add(x); Sort: Collections.sort(b); Concat: arr[idx++]=x;
